comment: Queue seed campaigns with committed, pre-generated AI assessments. Committing assessment JSON keeps seeding free and deterministic (no API calls). Evidence quotes are verbatim substrings of the cited field. 'class' is documentation only and is never stored or shown.

campaigns.title: Rebuild the Al-Noor Community Center kitchen
campaigns.description: Our community kitchen at Al-Noor Community Center in Columbus, Ohio has served free Friday meals for eleven years. In March a burst pipe flooded the kitchen and we lost most of our equipment. We have a written quote from Hardin Restaurant Supply for $14,200 covering two commercial ranges, a walk-in cooler compressor, and stainless prep tables, plus $4,300 from Mendel Brothers Plumbing for repiping. The center's board has approved the repair plan and our 501(c)(3) documents and both quotes are available to any donor on request. Every meal we serve is cooked by volunteers; your help puts us back in the kitchen by Ramadan.

output.summary: Established organizer with itemized contractor quotes that sum exactly to the goal; no risk signals.

campaigns.title: Help Maryam finish her final year of nursing school
campaigns.description: I am raising funds for my niece Maryam Hassan, a third-year nursing student at Wayne State University in Detroit. Her father passed away last spring and the family used their savings for funeral costs and rent. Maryam needs $4,800 to cover her final two semesters: $3,950 tuition balance (her student account statement is available on request) and $850 for clinical uniforms and licensing exam fees. She works twenty hours a week at a pharmacy but it is not enough to close the gap. Our masjid's education committee has verified the family's situation and will receive the funds directly.

output.summary: Specific, verifiable education costs with third-party verification by a masjid committee; zakat claim is consistent with a family in financial need.

campaigns.title: Winter coats for 200 refugee children in Dearborn
campaigns.description: Salaam Relief Network is buying winter coats, boots, and gloves for 200 newly arrived refugee children in Dearborn, Michigan before the cold sets in. We have negotiated a bulk price of $38 per child with Hanover Outfitters, which covers a coat, boots, and a glove-and-hat set ($7,600 total), plus $1,400 for sorting, sizing days, and delivery to three resettlement apartment complexes we already serve. We are a registered 501(c)(3) (EIN available on request) and have run this drive for five winters. Donations are zakat-eligible: every family we serve is within 130% of the federal poverty line.

output.summary: Registered organization with a five-year track record, per-unit pricing that sums to the goal, and a zakat claim grounded in stated poverty criteria.

campaigns.title: Masjid Al-Rahma roof repair after April storm
campaigns.description: The April 14 hailstorm tore through the roof membrane over our prayer hall at Masjid Al-Rahma in Tulsa. We have patched the leaks with tarps, but the engineer's inspection found the decking under two sections must be replaced before winter. Crown Roofing's written estimate is $29,400; our insurance approved only $6,000 because of the roof's age, leaving $23,400. The masjid board voted to repair rather than rebuild, and we are publishing the estimate, the insurance letter, and monthly spending updates on our website. Jumu'ah attendance is over 400 — help us keep the roof over their heads.

output.summary: Goal equals the contractor estimate minus the disclosed insurance payout, with documents published; long-standing institutional organizer.

campaigns.title: Emergency heart surgery for my daughter Amira
campaigns.description: My daughter Amira is six years old and was born with a ventricular septal defect. Her cardiologist says she needs corrective surgery within the next few months. We are working with the hospital's financial aid office, which quoted us $12,000 as our out-of-pocket responsibility after assistance. I am a single father working two jobs and I cannot cover it alone. Anything you give goes straight to the hospital billing department. Please keep Amira in your prayers and share this with anyone who can help us reach our goal.

output.summary: Plausible medical story, but the goal is nearly four times the stated out-of-pocket hospital quote.

evidence.claim: The stated medical need is far below the campaign goal
evidence.quote: quoted us $12,000 as our out-of-pocket responsibility after assistance

evidence.claim: The campaign goal exceeds the stated need by $33,000 with no explanation
Brand-new organizer account with no campaign history

risk_factors: Goal amount is 3.75x the stated hospital quote with no explanation for the difference
Account was created less than a month before the campaign
No hospital or cardiologist is named, so the quoted figure cannot be verified
mitigating_factors: The congenital diagnosis is specific and medically plausible
Organizer states funds go directly to hospital billing
output.reasoning: The medical story itself is plausible and specific, but the only dollar figure in the description accounts for roughly a quarter of the goal. Legitimate campaigns usually explain costs beyond the hospital bill (travel, lost wages); this one does not. A reviewer should request the hospital's financial aid letter before this campaign is allowed to collect.

campaigns.title: School kits for 150 orphans this Eid
campaigns.description: Alhamdulillah, with your support we have done this for three years running. This Eid we are preparing school kits for 150 orphans in our care: each kit has a backpack, uniform, shoes, and a full stationery set, sourced in bulk at $38 per child. Last year's distribution reached three cities and every donor received photos. We handle everything ourselves so there are no administration fees, and one hundred percent of what you give reaches a child. Donations qualify as zakat. Give your zakat where you can see it work.

output.summary: Itemized kit cost accounts for about a fifth of the goal, the operating organization is never named, and the organizer has run four similar campaigns in seven months.

evidence.claim: The itemized cost does not support the goal
evidence.quote: school kits for 150 orphans in our care: each kit has a backpack, uniform, shoes, and a full stationery set, sourced in bulk at $38 per child

evidence.claim: 150 kits at $38 each totals $5,700, yet the goal is $28,000
No organization, orphanage, or registration is named that could be verified
Four prior campaigns on a seven-month-old account, one previously flagged

risk_factors: Stated per-kit cost times beneficiary count covers only about 20% of the goal
Organizer has run four similar campaigns in seven months, one previously flagged
"Orphans in our care" implies an institution but none is named or registered
The zakat claim rests on the same unverifiable orphan-care claim
mitigating_factors: Per-kit pricing and donor photos suggest some real past activity
Tone and structure are consistent with legitimate seasonal drives
output.reasoning: The campaign borrows the shape of a legitimate annual drive, but the arithmetic gap between the itemized kits and the goal is large and unexplained, and the cadence of similar campaigns from a young, previously-flagged account fits a recycled-story pattern. A reviewer should request the operating organization's name and registration and last year's distribution records before approval.

campaigns.title: URGENT: my family will be on the street in 48 hours
campaigns.description: I don't have time to make this pretty. The bank has given us 48 hours before they change the locks on our home. I have three children and my wife is pregnant. If we do not raise $85,000 by Friday we lose everything we have ever worked for. I can't share the bank documents here for privacy reasons but I swear on everything that this is real. Every minute you wait is a minute my children get closer to sleeping in our van. Please, if you are reading this, do not scroll past. Allah sees what you do. Donate now and share, there is no time.

output.summary: Extreme manufactured deadline, an explicit refusal to provide any verifiable detail, and a large round goal from a four-day-old account.

evidence.claim: An artificial 48-hour deadline is used as pressure
evidence.quote: The bank has given us 48 hours before they change the locks

evidence.claim: The organizer pre-emptively declines to provide documentation
evidence.quote: I can't share the bank documents here for privacy reasons

evidence.claim: Donors are coerced emotionally and religiously
evidence.quote: do not scroll past. Allah sees what you do

evidence.claim: Large round goal with no breakdown of any kind
The account is four days old with no history

risk_factors: Countdown pressure is the campaign's central device
$85,000 with zero itemization; mortgage defaults are negotiated with lenders over months, not resolved by crowdfunding in 48 hours
Pre-emptive excuse for why nothing can be verified
Brand-new account with no platform history
output.reasoning: Nearly every sentence is a pressure device and the single concrete number is an unitemized $85,000. The pre-emptive explanation for why no document can be shared is characteristic of fabricated emergencies. Recommend escalation with account review rather than a quiet rejection, in case other campaigns share this device pattern.

campaigns.title: Guaranteed cure for my wife's cancer at overseas clinic
campaigns.description: Doctors here gave up on my wife but we found a clinic in Europe that guarantees complete remission with a new treatment that hospitals don't want you to know about. The clinic has a 100% success rate and has already reserved her place, but we must wire $120,000 within ten days or my sister loses her spot. We will sell our car but it is not enough. I cannot say the clinic's name publicly because the medical industry will pressure them. God will repay every donor ten times over. Please hurry, her life is literally in your hands.

output.summary: Guaranteed-cure claim from an unnameable clinic, a wire-transfer deadline, and the patient changes from wife to sister mid-story.

evidence.claim: A medically impossible guarantee is the campaign's premise
A perfect success rate is claimed
evidence.quote: The clinic has a 100% success rate

evidence.claim: The patient's identity changes mid-story
evidence.quote: or my sister loses her spot

evidence.claim: The clinic cannot be verified by design
evidence.quote: I cannot say the clinic's name publicly

evidence.claim: Two prior flags on a sixteen-day-old account

risk_factors: "Guaranteed" oncology outcomes do not exist; this is a hallmark of treatment scams
Wire-transfer framing with a ten-day deadline
The beneficiary is 'my wife' in one sentence and 'my sister' in another
An unnameable clinic forecloses all verification
Account is sixteen days old and already flagged twice
output.reasoning: This combines the strongest fraud signatures in the rubric — impossible guarantees, identity inconsistency, and enforced unverifiability — on an account that is two weeks old with two prior flags. Escalate for account-level action and pattern matching against the previously flagged campaigns.

campaigns.title: Help us get through the month after the fire
campaigns.description: Two weeks ago there was a fire in our apartment building and we lost almost everything in the smoke and water damage. The Red Cross put us in a hotel for three nights and since then we have been staying with my cousin, six of us in a two-bedroom apartment. I am asking for help with one month of rent and a deposit on a new place, clothes for my kids for school, and replacing my work boots and tools so I can keep doing electrical jobs. I have never asked for help like this before and I don't have paperwork from the fire department yet. May Allah reward anyone who helps us.

output.summary: Plausible, modest fire-recovery request, but with no documentation and a new account it is hard to distinguish from fabricated urgency.

evidence.claim: No documentation is currently available
evidence.quote: I don't have paperwork from the fire department yet

evidence.claim: The account is new with no prior activity
The request is itemized and modest, consistent with real displacement
evidence.quote: one month of rent and a deposit on a new place

risk_factors: Nothing in the campaign can currently be verified — no fire report, address, or named building
Account is seven weeks old with no prior activity
mitigating_factors: The goal is modest and itemized toward concrete recovery needs rather than a round number
The Red Cross hotel placement is a checkable detail if the organizer is asked
Requesting work tools suggests intent to return to self-sufficiency
output.reasoning: There is insufficient information to distinguish an urgent legitimate need from fabricated urgency: every detail is plausible and the modest, itemized goal weighs against fraud, but nothing is independently verifiable yet. This is exactly the kind of case a human should decide — recommend escalating with a request for the Red Cross case number or the fire department report rather than approving or rejecting on the current record.

campaigns.title: Zakat for my brother's dialysis transport and care
campaigns.description: My brother Idris has been on dialysis three times a week since his kidneys failed last year. Medicaid covers the sessions but not the medical transport he needs to get to them, his renal diet, or the months of bills that piled up while he could not work. I am asking for $15,000 to cover a year of transport at roughly $220 a week, help with his food costs, and part of the debt. He lives alone in Newark and I am his only family nearby. I believe this qualifies for zakat as he has no income right now, and I will share receipts with any donor who asks.

output.summary: Coherent costs for a plausible chronic-care need, but the zakat eligibility and the underlying situation rest on unverified facts.

evidence.claim: Zakat eligibility depends on an income status that is asserted but not evidenced
evidence.quote: I believe this qualifies for zakat as he has no income right now

evidence.claim: The cost arithmetic is broadly consistent with the goal
evidence.quote: a year of transport at roughly $220 a week

evidence.claim: The campaign is presented to donors as zakat-eligible

risk_factors: The beneficiary's income and debt status — the entire basis of the zakat claim — cannot be verified from the campaign
Mixed use of funds (transport, food, unspecified debt) blurs how much goes where
mitigating_factors: The weekly transport figure times 52 weeks roughly matches the goal
Organizer offers receipts to any donor who asks
Account is over a year old with one prior campaign and no flags
output.reasoning: The need is plausible and the arithmetic is honest, but the zakat designation raises the bar: donors fulfilling a religious obligation rely on the platform's eligibility judgment, and the facts that make this zakat-eligible (no income, qualifying debt) are asserted without evidence. Recommend escalation to request income documentation before the zakat label is shown to donors.

live_campaigns.title: Winter heating fund for Bosnian refugee families in Utica
live_campaigns.description: Eleven resettled families on Utica's east side share aging furnaces that failed inspection this fall. Working with the Mohawk Valley Resource Center for Refugees, we have a written quote from Bartell Heating to repair or replace six units before the first freeze. MVRCR is administering the funds and every receipt is posted to our updates page.

output.summary: Named partner org administering funds, written contractor quote, established organizer; no risk signals.

review.note: Verified MVRCR partnership and contractor quote. Zakat-eligible need is clear. Approved.

live_campaigns.title: Rebuild Masjid As-Salam's wudu facilities after the flood
live_campaigns.description: June flash flooding ruined the ablution area and two restrooms at our 200-family mosque in Poughkeepsie. The board approved a repair plan and we have a $26,400 bid from Hudson Valley Plumbing; insurance is covering the difference above this campaign's goal. Plans and the bid are available to any donor on request.

output.summary: Long-standing organizer, board-approved repair with an itemized bid; goal sits below the total cost. No concerns.

review.note: Board-approved scope, itemized bid, insurance covers the remainder. Approved.

live_campaigns.title: Tuition gap for Amina's final year of nursing school
live_campaigns.description: Amina is a first-generation student one year from her RN at SUNY Broome. A reduction in her father's work hours left a $7,800 tuition gap for her final two semesters. The financial-aid office can confirm enrollment and the outstanding balance; funds will be paid directly to the bursar.

output.summary: Specific institution and verifiable balance, funds paid to bursar; first-time organizer is the only mild uncertainty.

review.note: Direct-to-bursar disbursement and verifiable enrollment offset the first-time-organizer flag. Zakat-eligible. Approved.

live_campaigns.title: Emergency surgery for baby Zayd's congenital heart defect
live_campaigns.description: Zayd was born with tetralogy of Fallot and needs corrective surgery at Boston Children's Hospital. After insurance, the family faces $14,200 in out-of-pocket costs for travel, lodging near the hospital, and the deductible. The hospital social worker can verify the treatment plan and cost estimate.

output.summary: Named hospital and condition, itemized post-insurance costs, verifiable via hospital social worker. No fraud signals.

review.note: Named facility and condition with a verifiable cost breakdown. Approved.
